question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getOutstandingAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user query outstanding airdrops for any arbitrary account ID via the `{id}` path variable without any authentication or ownership verification, thereby learning the full set of pending token transfers initiated by a victim sender account, enabling targeted social engineering or front-running attacks?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getPendingAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user enumerate all pending airdrops destined for any receiver account by supplying an arbitrary `{id}` path variable with no session token or signature required, exposing the receiver's full pending token claim list without the receiver's approval or knowledge?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: processRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user bypass all ownership checks in `processRequest()` because there is no caller-identity binding between the HTTP request and the `EntityIdParameter id`, allowing any user to impersonate any account ID and retrieve its airdrop state without the account owner's approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getOutstandingAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a victim's EVM address as the `{id}` path variable (resolved via `EntityIdEvmAddressParameter`) to retrieve all outstanding airdrops sent by that victim without the victim's consent, leaking token IDs, receiver IDs, and serial numbers?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getOutstandingAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a victim's base32 alias as the `{id}` path variable (resolved via `EntityIdAliasParameter`) to retrieve all outstanding airdrops for that account without any signature or approval from the account owner, leaking sensitive financial relationships?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: processRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user enumerate all accounts that have outstanding airdrops by iterating numeric account IDs in the `{id}` path variable, since `processRequest()` performs no rate-limiting or authorization check, enabling mass surveillance of token transfer activity across the network?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getOutstandingAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user use the `receiver.id` query parameter with a range operator (e.g., `gte:0`) to retrieve all outstanding airdrops for a victim sender across all possible receivers without the sender's approval, exposing the complete financial graph of that account?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getPendingAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user use the `sender.id` query parameter with a range operator to retrieve all pending airdrops for a victim receiver across all possible senders without the receiver's approval, enabling an attacker to map the victim's entire incoming airdrop portfolio?
